title: Paraboea minor
taxonomy: Paraboea minor is classified within the plant family Gesneriaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the 'African Violet' family. This family is part of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further classified within the clade Asteridae. The clade Asteridae is a group of flowering plants that also includes families such as the Campanulaceae and the Plantaginaceae. Overall, the classification of Paraboea minor reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Paraboea minor is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the African violet family, that is native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Paraboea minor include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
